---
title: "3_Models"
author: "Arturo Bertero"
date: "2026-02-09"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#clean
rm(list = ls())

library("pacman")
p_load(tidyverse, here, haven, countrycode, sjPlot, lme4, survey, broom,
       marginaleffects, ggeffects)

```


# Input

```{r}
# Export to RDS
lon_turn_df = readRDS(here("Input", "data_clean.rds"))
```

# Processing

```{r}
# # weights
# ess_design <- svydesign(
#   ids = ~psu, 
#   strata = ~stratum, 
#   weights = ~anweight, 
#   data = lon_turn_df, 
#   nest = TRUE
# )
```

## Country models

### M1 and M2: lon and si in isolation

```{r}
# 1. Run the Models across all countries
m1_m2_results <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(
    lon_n = as.numeric(lon),
    si_n  = as.numeric(si_levels)
  ) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    # M1: Effect of Loneliness
    model1 = map(data, ~glm(vote ~ lon_n + gender + age_centered + educ + hhincome, 
                             data = .x, family = binomial)),
    # M2: Effect of Social Isolation
    model2 = map(data, ~glm(vote ~ si_n + gender + age_centered + educ + hhincome, 
                             data = .x, family = binomial))
  ) %>%
  # 2. Extract and Tidy (Converting to Odds Ratios)
  mutate(
    tidy1 = map(model1, ~tidy(.x, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE)),
    tidy2 = map(model2, ~tidy(.x, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  select(cntry, tidy1, tidy2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("tidy"), names_to = "model_source", values_to = "estimates") %>%
  unnest(estimates) %>%
  filter(term %in% c("lon_n", "si_n")) %>%
  mutate(
    predictor = if_else(term == "lon_n", "Loneliness (M1)", "Social Isolation (M2)"),
    significant = conf.low > 1 | conf.high < 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3. Simplified Plotting Function
# We use reorder(cntry, estimate) to sort by the Odds Ratio magnitude
p_m1_m2_simple <- ggplot(m1_m2_results, aes(x = reorder(cntry, estimate), 
                                            y = estimate, color = significant)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~predictor, scales = "free", ncol = 1) + # Use free scales to keep order meaningful
  coord_flip() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey70", "TRUE" = "#d7191c")) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Turnout Odds Ratios: Loneliness vs. Social Isolation",
    subtitle = "Odds Ratio < 1 indicates a negative effect. Red = Significant (p < .05).",
    x = "Country", 
    y = "Odds Ratio (Exponentiated Coefficients)",
    caption = "Controls: Age, Gender, Education, Income. Estimated separately per country."
  )

# 4. Save
ggsave(here("Output", "Models", "M1_M2.png"), 
       p_m1_m2_simple, width = 10, height = 12)
```

### M3: sil_type

```{r}
# 1. Run the Models and Get Predictions (Same logic as before, but keeping data tidy)
m3_probs_clean <- lon_turn_df %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(model = map(data, ~glm(vote ~ sil_type + gender + age_centered + educ + hhincome, 
                                 data = .x, family = binomial))) %>%
  mutate(preds = map(model, function(m) {
    pred_data <- expand.grid(
      sil_type = levels(lon_turn_df$sil_type),
      gender   = levels(lon_turn_df$gender)[1],
      educ     = levels(lon_turn_df$educ)[1],
      hhincome = levels(lon_turn_df$hhincome)[1],
      age_centered = 0
    )
    p_link <- predict(m, newdata = pred_data, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)
    pred_data %>%
      mutate(
        prob  = plogis(p_link$fit),
        lower = plogis(p_link$fit - 1.96 * p_link$se.fit),
        upper = plogis(p_link$fit + 1.96 * p_link$se.fit)
      )
  })) %>%
  select(cntry, preds) %>%
  unnest(preds)

# 2. Define Plotting Function
# This creates a 'staircase' plot for each country facet
plot_m3_by_country <- function(data_subset, batch_name) {
  ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = sil_type, y = prob, color = sil_type)) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), size = 0.6) +
    facet_wrap(~cntry, ncol = 3) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1.0)) +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu", direction = -1) + # Red for High/High, Blue for Low/Low
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_blank(), # Legend explains the levels
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("The Turnout Gap: Loneliness & Isolation -", batch_name),
      subtitle = "Predicted Probabilities of Voting. Comparing the 4 typology groups within each country.",
      x = NULL, y = "Prob. of Voting",
      color = "Typology Group"
    )
}

# 3. Batch and Save
unique_countries <- unique(m3_probs_clean$cntry)
p_m3_b1 <- plot_m3_by_country(filter(m3_probs_clean, cntry %in% unique_countries[1:15]), "Batch 1")
p_m3_b2 <- plot_m3_by_country(filter(m3_probs_clean, cntry %in% unique_countries[16:30]), "Batch 2")

ggsave(here("Output", "Models", "M3_Probabilities_Batch1.png"), p_m3_b1, width = 12, height = 15)
ggsave(here("Output", "Models", "M3_Probabilities_Batch2.png"), p_m3_b2, width = 12, height = 15)
```

### M4: lon + si

```{r}
#1. Run M4 for each country
# vote = lon_n + si_n + controls
m4_results <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(
    lon_n = as.numeric(lon),
    si_n  = as.numeric(si_levels)
  ) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(model = map(data, ~glm(vote ~ lon_n + si_n + gender + age_centered + educ + hhincome, 
                                 data = .x, family = binomial))) %>%
  # 2. Extract and Tidy (Exponentiate for Odds Ratios)
  mutate(tidy_m = map(model, ~tidy(.x, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE))) %>%
  unnest(tidy_m) %>%
  filter(term %in% c("lon_n", "si_n")) %>%
  mutate(
    predictor = if_else(term == "lon_n", "Loneliness Penalty (adj.)", "Social Isolation Penalty (adj.)"),
    significant = conf.low > 1 | conf.high < 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3. Create the Odds Ratio Plot
# Sorted by magnitude within each facet to show the gradient of the 'penalty'
p_m4_odds <- ggplot(m4_results, aes(x = reorder(cntry, estimate), y = estimate, color = significant)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~predictor, scales = "free", ncol = 1) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey70", "TRUE" = "#d7191c")) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "M4: Independent Turnout Penalties (Additive Model)",
    subtitle = "Odds Ratios < 1 indicate lower turnout. Each effect is adjusted for the other.",
    x = "Country", 
    y = "Odds Ratio (Exponentiated Coefficients)",
    caption = "Controls: Age, Gender, Education, Income. Red = Significant (p < .05)."
  )

# 4. Save
ggsave(here("Output", "Models", "M4_Additive_OddsRatios.png"), 
       p_m4_odds, width = 10, height = 12)
```

### M5 lon interact with si

```{r}
# 1. Run M5 Interaction Model for each country
m5_interaction_results <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(
    lon_n = as.numeric(lon),
    si_n  = as.numeric(si_levels)
  ) %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(model = map(data, ~glm(vote ~ lon_n * si_n + gender + age_centered + educ + hhincome, 
                                 data = .x, family = binomial))) %>%
  # 2. Extract only the Interaction Term
  mutate(tidy_m = map(model, ~tidy(.x, conf.int = TRUE))) %>%
  unnest(tidy_m) %>%
  filter(term == "lon_n:si_n") %>%
  mutate(significant = conf.low > 0 | conf.high < 0) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3. Create the Significance Plot
# Sorted by magnitude to see which countries have the strongest interaction
p_m5_interaction_sig <- ggplot(m5_interaction_results, aes(x = reorder(cntry, estimate), 
                                                           y = estimate, 
                                                           color = significant)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), size = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey70", "TRUE" = "#e41a1c")) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Significance of the Double Burden Interaction (M5)",
    subtitle = "Coefficient for lon_n:si_n. Negative = Loneliness hurts more as Isolation increases.",
    x = "Country", 
    y = "Interaction Coefficient (Log-Odds Estimate)",
    caption = "Red = Significant interaction (p < .05). Controls included in each model."
  )

# 4. Save
ggsave(here("Output", "Models", "M5_Interaction_Significance.png"), 
       p_m5_interaction_sig, width = 8, height = 10)
```

## Multilevel model

```{r}
# Ensure numeric versions and factors are ready
mlm_df <- lon_turn_df %>%
  mutate(
    lon_n = as.numeric(lon),
    si_n  = as.numeric(si_levels)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(vote), !is.na(lon_n), !is.na(si_n))

# 2. Fit the "Final Boss" Model
# Random intercept for country + Random slopes for Loneliness and Isolation
# We include the interaction lon_n * si_n to test the Double Burden average
m_final_mlm <- glmer(
  vote ~ lon_n * si_n + gender + age_centered + educ + hhincome + 
         (1 | cntry) + (0 + lon_n | cntry) + (0 + si_n | cntry), 
  data = mlm_df, 
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(
    optimizer = "bobyqa", 
    optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e5)
  )
)

# 3. Print the Summary Table (Professional Formatting)
tab_model(m_final_mlm, 
          show.re.var = TRUE, 
          pred.labels = c("Intercept", "Loneliness (Numeric)", "Social Isolation (Numeric)", 
                          "Gender (Ref: Male)", "Age (Centered)", "Education", "Income",
                          "Interaction: Lon x SI"),
          title = "Table 1: Multilevel Model of Turnout in Europe",
          file = here("Output", "Models", "Final_MLM_Table.html"))

# 4. Plot the Interaction Marginal Effects (The "Verdict" Plot)
# This shows the European average for the interaction
mlm_preds <- predict_response(m_final_mlm, terms = c("lon_n", "si_n"))

p_final_verdict <- plot(mlm_preds) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:3, labels = c("None", "Some", "Most/All")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1.0)) +
  labs(
    title = "The European Average: Loneliness x Social Isolation",
    subtitle = "Predicted Probabilities from the Multilevel Model (All 30 countries pooled)",
    x = "Loneliness Frequency",
    y = "Prob. of Voting",
    color = "Isolation Level"
  )

# 5. Save the Verdict
ggsave(here("Output", "Models", "Final_MLM_Interaction_Plot.png"), 
       p_final_verdict, width = 8, height = 6)
```
